---
title: 'Functional annotation using EnTAP'
teaching: 10
exercises: 2
---

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: questions 

- to do1
- to do2
- to do3

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: objectives

- to do1
- to do2
- to do3

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

## 01_Setup

Installing EnTAP and Dependencies
Preparing Reference Databases (UniProt, KEGG, GO, etc.)
Configuring EnTAP for Functional Annotation

## 02_Running EnTAP

Preparing Input Data (GFF, FASTA, Transcript Files)
Running EnTAP Pipeline
Running EnTAP with Custom Databases (if applicable)

## 03_Results and Outputs

### Interpreting Functional Annotations

- Gene Ontology (GO Terms)
- KEGG Pathways
- InterPro Domains

### Assessing Annotation Completeness

- Comparison with Reference Annotations
- Functional Assignment Statistics
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: keypoints 

- to do1
- to do2
- to do3

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
